title: Schwabing-Freimann

infobox.value: (German pronunciation: [[ˈʃvaːbɪŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Schwabing.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Schwabing.wav")) is a [borough](/wiki/Borough "Borough") in the northern part of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), the [capital](/wiki/Capital_(political) "Capital (political)") of the [German](/wiki/Germany "Germany") state of [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria"). It is part of the city borough 4 (**Schwabing-West**) and the city borough 12 (**Schwabing-Freimann**). The population of Schwabing is estimated at 100,000, making it one of the largest districts of Munich. The main boulevard is [Leopoldstraße](/wiki/Leopoldstra%C3%9Fe "Leopoldstraße").[[1]](#cite_note-DK_2015-1) (For further information on the Munich boroughs, see [Boroughs of Munich](/wiki/Boroughs_of_Munich "Boroughs of Munich").)

[ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9780241196014](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9780241196014 "Special:BookSources/9780241196014") – via Google Books.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [48°10′02″N 11°35′10″E﻿ / ﻿48.16722°N 11.58611°E﻿ / 48.16722; 11.58611](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Schwabing&params=48_10_02_N_11_35_10_E_region:DE-BY_type:city_source:dewiki)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Schwabing-Freimann](/w/index.php?title=Schwabing-Freimann&redirect=no "Schwabing-Freimann"))

Borough in the northern part of Munich, Germany

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/Siegestor_bei_Nacht.JPG/250px-Siegestor_bei_Nacht.JPG)](/wiki/File:Siegestor_bei_Nacht.JPG)

The [Siegestor](/wiki/Siegestor "Siegestor") (front), on [Leopoldstraße](/wiki/Leopoldstra%C3%9Fe "Leopoldstraße") between Munich's Maxvorstadt and Schwabing

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/52/Walking_Man_Munich_Schwabing.jpg/250px-Walking_Man_Munich_Schwabing.jpg)](/wiki/File:Walking_Man_Munich_Schwabing.jpg)

[Walking Man](/wiki/Walking_Man_(Borofsky) "Walking Man (Borofsky)") at Leopoldstraße

**Schwabing** (German pronunciation: [[ˈʃvaːbɪŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Schwabing.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Schwabing.wav")) is a [borough](/wiki/Borough "Borough") in the northern part of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), the [capital](/wiki/Capital_(political) "Capital (political)") of the [German](/wiki/Germany "Germany") state of [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria"). It is part of the city borough 4 (**Schwabing-West**) and the city borough 12 (**Schwabing-Freimann**). The population of Schwabing is estimated at 100,000, making it one of the largest districts of Munich. The main boulevard is [Leopoldstraße](/wiki/Leopoldstra%C3%9Fe "Leopoldstraße").[[1]](#cite_note-DK_2015-1) (For further information on the Munich boroughs, see [Boroughs of Munich](/wiki/Boroughs_of_Munich "Boroughs of Munich").)

## Overview

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Schwabing&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7a/Schwasi7_Umzug.jpg/250px-Schwasi7_Umzug.jpg)](/wiki/File:Schwasi7_Umzug.jpg)

2011 before the demolition of the [Schwabinger 7](/wiki/Schwabinger_7 "Schwabinger 7") bar and redevelopment of the area shown in the photograph

Schwabing was a village, with [a church](/wiki/St._Sylvester,_Schwabing "St. Sylvester, Schwabing") documented in the 14th century.

Schwabing used to be famous as Munich's [bohemian](/wiki/Bohemianism "Bohemianism") quarter, but has lost much of this reputation due to strong gentrification in the last decades.[[2]](#cite_note-Hemminger_2012-2) A popular location is the *[Englischer Garten](/wiki/Englischer_Garten "Englischer Garten")*, or English Garden, one of the world's largest public parks. Other not so commonly known parks in Schwabing are [Leopoldpark](/w/index.php?title=Leopoldpark&action=edit&redlink=1 "Leopoldpark (page does not exist)"), [Petuelpark](/wiki/Petuelpark "Petuelpark") and [Biotop am Ackermannbogen](/w/index.php?title=Biotop_am_Ackermannbogen&action=edit&redlink=1 "Biotop am Ackermannbogen (page does not exist)").

The main buildings of Munich's largest [universities](/wiki/University "University"), [LMU Munich](/wiki/LMU_Munich "LMU Munich") and the [Technical University of Munich](/wiki/Technical_University_of_Munich "Technical University of Munich") and [Academy of Fine Arts](/wiki/Academy_of_Fine_Arts,_Munich "Academy of Fine Arts, Munich") are situated in the nearby [Maxvorstadt](/wiki/Maxvorstadt "Maxvorstadt"). A student housing area called "[Studentenstadt](/wiki/Studentenstadt "Studentenstadt")" (literally, "student city") is located in the north of Schwabing.

The [gentrification](/wiki/Gentrification "Gentrification") of Schwabing and various construction projects led to various protests around the year 2011.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The Schwabinger Tor urban redevelopment includes more than 200 apartments, 50 offices, a 5-star hotel, and restaurants.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Schwabing&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Culture")]

Schwabing in the 1970s: The Yellow Submarine discotheque and the futuristic Schwabylon shopping center

### Bohemia since 1890

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Schwabing&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Bohemia since 1890")]

Schwabing became very famous especially during the reign of Prince Regent [Luitpold](/wiki/Luitpold,_Prince_Regent_of_Bavaria "Luitpold, Prince Regent of Bavaria") when numerous artists like [Ludwig Ganghofer](/wiki/Ludwig_Ganghofer "Ludwig Ganghofer"), [Heinrich Mann](/wiki/Heinrich_Mann "Heinrich Mann"), [Thomas Mann](/wiki/Thomas_Mann "Thomas Mann"), [Oskar Panizza](/wiki/Oskar_Panizza "Oskar Panizza"), [Otto Julius Bierbaum](/wiki/Otto_Julius_Bierbaum "Otto Julius Bierbaum"), [Frank Wedekind](/wiki/Frank_Wedekind "Frank Wedekind"), [Ernst von Wolzogen](/wiki/Ernst_von_Wolzogen "Ernst von Wolzogen"), [Gustav Meyrink](/wiki/Gustav_Meyrink "Gustav Meyrink"), [Rainer Maria Rilke](/wiki/Rainer_Maria_Rilke "Rainer Maria Rilke"), [Isolde Kurz](/wiki/Isolde_Kurz "Isolde Kurz"), [Ludwig Thoma](/wiki/Ludwig_Thoma "Ludwig Thoma"), [Max Halbe](/wiki/Max_Halbe "Max Halbe"), [Annette Kolb](/wiki/Annette_Kolb "Annette Kolb"), [Stefan George](/wiki/Stefan_George "Stefan George"), [Karl Wolfskehl](/wiki/Karl_Wolfskehl "Karl Wolfskehl"), [Ludwig Klages](/wiki/Ludwig_Klages "Ludwig Klages"), [Roda Roda](/wiki/Roda_Roda "Roda Roda"), [Christian Morgenstern](/wiki/Christian_Morgenstern "Christian Morgenstern"), [Max Dauthendey](/wiki/Max_Dauthendey "Max Dauthendey"), [Mechtilde Lichnowsky](/wiki/Mechtilde_Lichnowsky "Mechtilde Lichnowsky"), [Lion Feuchtwanger](/wiki/Lion_Feuchtwanger "Lion Feuchtwanger"), [Leonhard Frank](/wiki/Leonhard_Frank "Leonhard Frank"), [Joachim Ringelnatz](/wiki/Joachim_Ringelnatz "Joachim Ringelnatz"), [Claire Goll](/wiki/Claire_Goll "Claire Goll"), [Oskar Maria Graf](/wiki/Oskar_Maria_Graf "Oskar Maria Graf"), [Hugo Ball](/wiki/Hugo_Ball "Hugo Ball"), [Hermann Kesten](/wiki/Hermann_Kesten "Hermann Kesten"), [Thomas Theodor Heine](/wiki/Thomas_Theodor_Heine "Thomas Theodor Heine"), [Olaf Gulbransson](/wiki/Olaf_Gulbransson "Olaf Gulbransson"), [Bruno Paul](/wiki/Bruno_Paul "Bruno Paul"), [Eduard Thöny](/wiki/Eduard_Th%C3%B6ny "Eduard Thöny") and [Rudolf Wilke](/wiki/Rudolf_Wilke "Rudolf Wilke") lived or worked there. [Lenin](/wiki/Lenin "Lenin") was a resident of Schwabing for some years, as was noted psychoanalyst and bohemian [Otto Gross](/wiki/Otto_Gross "Otto Gross").[[5]](#cite_note-5) The Countess [Fanny zu Reventlow](/wiki/Fanny_zu_Reventlow "Fanny zu Reventlow") was known as "The Bohemian Countess of Schwabing".

### Famous 60s and 70s Scene

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Schwabing&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Famous 60s and 70s Scene")]

In the 1960s and 1970s Schwabing became a hotspot for the flower power and 1968 movements as well as an internationally renowned party district with legendary clubs such as [Big Apple](/wiki/Big_Apple_(club) "Big Apple (club)"), PN hit-house, Domicile, Hot Club, Piper Club, Tiffany, Germany's first large-scale discotheque [Blow Up](/wiki/Blow_Up_(club) "Blow Up (club)") and the underwater nightclub [Yellow Submarine](/wiki/Yellow_Submarine_(club) "Yellow Submarine (club)"),[[6]](#cite_note-mjunikdisco-6) as well as many bars such as [Schwabinger 7](/wiki/Schwabinger_7 "Schwabinger 7"), Drugstore and Schwabinger Podium. From the active nightlife during this time, the district became known as "Schwabingbang". The [Schwabinger Krawalle](/w/index.php?title=Schwabinger_Krawalle&action=edit&redlink=1 "Schwabinger Krawalle (page does not exist)") unrests of 1962 were a prelude for the student protests of 1968. In the last decades Schwabing has lost much of its nightlife activity, mainly due to gentrification and the resulting high rents. It has become the city's most coveted and expensive residential district, attracting affluent citizens with little interest in partying.
